lesson_title: Daily Routines and Free Time: Listening Lesson 4
learning_objective: Learners can name, identify, ask and answer basic questions in a listening task about family.
cefr_outcome: Learners can understand and use familiar everyday expressions and very basic phrases for immediate needs.
learner_facing_explanation: In this lesson you will practise listening for daily routines and free time. You will learn useful language connected to family and practise there is/there are. By the end, you should be able to complete a short real-life task and explain your answer clearly at A1 level.
complete_teaching_input: Focus language: there is/there are. Vocabulary theme: family. The teacher introduces the target language in a meaningful context, models pronunciation and form, checks meaning with concept questions, and moves learners from controlled practice to freer use. At A1, learners should name, identify, ask and answer basic questions with increasing independence. The lesson should use board examples, short teacher modelling, pair practice, feedback and a final communicative task.
examples: Example 1: I can use there is/there are to talk about family.
Example 2: The learner gives a A1 answer with clear meaning and correct key language.
Example 3: The learner checks, improves and repeats the answer after feedback.
Example 4: The learner uses one linking phrase or support phrase suitable for A1.

interactive_practice.prompt: Choose the best sentence using there is/there are.
options: Correct use of there is/there are in a A1 sentence.
Incorrect word order with there is/there are.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Correct. The sentence uses there is/there are accurately.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check the form and meaning of there is/there are.

interactive_practice.prompt: Match the family phrase to its meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Good matching. You understand the phrase meaning.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Look again at the context and key words.

interactive_practice.prompt: Build a complete answer for the lesson task.
interactive_practice.model_answer: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand family.
interactive_practice.feedback_correct: Well done. Your sentence is complete and meaningful.
interactive_practice.feedback_incorrect: Check that your sentence has a subject, verb and clear meaning.

correct_answers.P3: I can explain my idea clearly because I understand family.

distractors: Wrong tense for there is/there are

instant_feedback.correct: Correct. Continue to the next step.
instant_feedback.partly_correct: Good start. Check accuracy and add one more detail.
instant_feedback.incorrect: Try again. Review the model and look at the key language.

teacher_notes: Begin with a quick diagnostic question. Model the target form there is/there are. Use choral repetition if pronunciation is important. Monitor pair work and record three common errors. Finish with delayed correction and one confidence-building success point.
homework_task: Write or record five sentences about family using language from this lesson.

assessment_task.task_title: A1 Listening Check - Daily Routines and Free Time
assessment_task.instructions: Complete a short listening task using there is/there are and vocabulary from family.
success_criteria: Uses there is/there are with reasonable accuracy
Responds at the expected CEFR level

criteria.name: Language control
criteria.descriptor: Uses there is/there are and sentence structure accurately.

criteria.name: Vocabulary range
criteria.descriptor: Uses appropriate words connected to family.

criteria.name: Fluency/coherence
criteria.descriptor: Organises ideas clearly for the level.

speaking_task: Speak for 60-120 seconds about family. Use at least two examples and one reason.
writing_task: Write a short A1 response about family. Include the target language: there is/there are.

listening_audio.script: Audio script for A1-M05-L04. Speaker one: Today we are practising family. Speaker two: I need to use there is/there are correctly. Speaker one: Listen carefully and choose the answer that best matches the meaning.

visual_activity_cards.title: There Is/There Are form
visual_activity_cards.alt_text: Visual grammar card for there is/there are
